prompt: Design a multi-stage training curriculum to teach a 7B base model (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct as the starting point) to respond in valid JSON-mode under context-window pressure (16k+ token inputs that include distractors, malformed JSON examples, and natural-language asides). The model must produce JSON matching a provided schema even when the user's request is conversational. Output a structured plan with stages; for each stage give: (1) a dataset description (source, format, approximate size), (2) a measurable success criterion before advancing to the next stage. Use ASCII output, no markdown headers required.

criteria.description: Covers prereqs (basic JSON parsing) + target capability + evaluation
Prereqs come before target; harder stages come after easier
Names concrete datasets / benchmarks / metrics, not vague descriptions
Curriculum, if executed, would plausibly produce the target capability

reference_solution:
Curriculum: teach Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct robust JSON-mode under context-window pressure.

Stage 1 - JSON syntax fluency.
  Dataset: 50k synthetic (prompt, response) pairs where prompt = a short request, response = a minimal well-formed JSON object. Source: generate via Llama-3.1-70B following a fixed schema bank. Length: 32-512 tokens.
  Success: 99% of held-out responses parse as valid JSON via json.loads on a 1k eval set.

Stage 2 - Schema-conditioned generation.
  Dataset: 20k (system-prompt, user, json-response) tuples where system-prompt includes a JSON schema and the response must validate against it. Source: GLAIVE function-calling dataset + a custom curation of 200 hand-written schemas.
  Success: 95% of responses validate against the provided schema via jsonschema.validate on 500 held-out examples.

Stage 3 - Distractor robustness.
  Dataset: 15k examples where the user prompt is wrapped in 4-8k tokens of conversational prefix (small-talk, role-play attempts, instructions to NOT respond in JSON). Inject 10 malformed JSON snippets in the context to test that the model doesn't copy them. Source: mix Anthropic HH-RLHF context-padding with custom adversarial prefixes.
  Success: 90% strict-schema-validation rate at 8k context; 85% at 16k context.

Stage 4 - Long-context schema retention.
  Dataset: 5k examples at 12-16k token inputs where the relevant schema is buried in the middle of the prompt (lost-in-the-middle stress test). Source: programmatically construct from MTEB long-doc corpora + schema injection.
  Success: 85% strict-schema validation across input positions 0/25/50/75/100% (no positional degradation beyond 5 pts).

Stage 5 - Negative example calibration.
  Dataset: 8k examples paired (good-json, bad-json) where bad is a near-miss (missing comma, trailing fields, wrong type). DPO format. Source: corrupt outputs from stage 2 via deterministic mutations.
  Success: implicit-reward margin > 1.0 on held-out pairs; downstream strict-validation up by 3+ pts vs stage 4 baseline.

Stage 6 - Final eval.
  Dataset: 1k held-out hard examples (not seen in any prior stage), 16k context, schemas from new domains (medical, legal, financial JSON shapes).
  Success: 80% strict-schema validation + 90% parses (even when validation fails) on this held-out set.
